vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF ingested from GitHub issue #1563

plan.title: Deterministic questions break when host UI renders structured prompts alphabetically

narratives.Description: Deterministic question flows can break when host-native structured UI renders choices with alphabetic affordances while Directive expects numbered menus. This story makes deterministic menus host-portable by requiring visible numeric labels and strict answer mapping.
narratives.Overview:
## Problem

Directive's deterministic-questions contract requires numbered menus with `Discuss` and `Back` as the final two options. In Cursor, an agent can accidentally use the structured question UI for a deterministic flow, but the client may render options as form choices / alphabetic affordances rather than as the canonical numbered menu.

That creates an ambiguity gap:

- Directive expects users to answer with the displayed number.
- A host UI may show alphabetic option affordances instead.
- If the user replies in chat with a letter like `d` or `b`, the structured question tool may report no selection.
- The agent may then incorrectly reinterpret the raw letter semantically, e.g. `d` as Defer/Discuss ambiguity or `b` as Back.

This breaks deterministic navigation and can cause the agent to take the wrong branch in triage/refinement flows.

## Observed Example

During `deft-directive-triage`, the canonical decision menu should be:

1. Accept
2. Reject
3. Defer
4. Needs-AC
5. Mark duplicate
6. Discuss
7. Back

The agent instead used Cursor's structured question UI. The user saw/used alphabetic list letters and replied `d` / `b`. The tool returned no structured answer, and the agent interpreted those letters outside the canonical contract.

No destructive triage action was written in this case, but the failure mode is real.

## Proposed Solution

Update Directive's deterministic-questions contract and affected skills to define a host-portable rendering rule:

- Deterministic flows MUST render the canonical numbered menu in plain chat text unless the host UI is known to preserve numeric option labels and return numeric selections.
- Agents MUST accept only the displayed number or exact option text for deterministic choices.
- Agents MUST NOT infer from alphabetic letters unless the displayed menu itself used those letters.
- `Discuss` and `Back` remain the final two options in the rendered menu.
- If a host-native structured question tool is used, the prompt must visibly include the canonical number in each option label, and fallback chat replies must be mapped only against those visible numbers/text.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `contracts/deterministic-questions.md` documents the host-UI portability rule.
- Triage/refinement/swarm/setup skills stop implying that structured question tools are automatically safe.
- The canonical triage action menu remains numbered and portable.
- A regression test or content check prevents deterministic menus from depending on non-numbered host UI affordances.
narratives.ImplementationPlan:
1. Update contracts/deterministic-questions.md and the affected skill guidance files so deterministic flows render canonical numbered menus in chat unless the host UI visibly preserves numeric option labels.
2. Add content-test verification in tests/content/test_deterministic_questions.py and focused skill tests for triage/refinement/swarm/setup language so alphabetic host affordances are not treated as canonical deterministic replies.
narratives.UserStory: As a Deft operator, I want deterministic menus to be host-portable, so that agents do not misinterpret alphabetic UI affordances as canonical choices.
narratives.Traces: Issue #1563; contracts/deterministic-questions.md; triage/refinement/swarm/setup deterministic menu guidance.

narrative.Acceptance: Given a deterministic choice flow, when the contract is followed, then the canonical numbered menu is visible in chat or preserved verbatim in host-native option labels.
narrative.Traces: Issue #1563; contracts/deterministic-questions.md; triage/refinement/swarm/setup deterministic menu guidance.

items.title: Fallback replies map only to visible choices

narrative.Acceptance: Given a user replies in chat, when the menu was numbered, then agents accept only the displayed number or exact option text and do not infer from unrelated letters like d or b.
narrative.Traces: Issue #1563; contracts/deterministic-questions.md; triage/refinement/swarm/setup deterministic menu guidance.

items.title: Affected skills stop treating structured UI as automatically safe

narrative.Acceptance: Given triage/refinement/swarm/setup skills discuss deterministic questions, then they require the portable rendering rule rather than assuming structured question tools preserve the contract.
narrative.Traces: Issue #1563; contracts/deterministic-questions.md; triage/refinement/swarm/setup deterministic menu guidance.

items.title: Content tests pin the portability rule

narrative.Acceptance: Given content tests run, then they fail if the deterministic question contract or affected skill guidance drops numeric labels, Discuss/Back finality, or strict fallback mapping.
narrative.Traces: Issue #1563; contracts/deterministic-questions.md; triage/refinement/swarm/setup deterministic menu guidance.

references.title: Issue #1563: Deterministic questions break when host UI renders structured prompts alphabetically

verify_commands: uv run pytest tests/content/test_deterministic_questions.py tests/content/test_triage_skill.py tests/content/test_refinement_skill.py tests/content/test_swarm_skill.py tests/content/test_interview_deterministic.py
expected_outputs: content tests assert host-portable numbered deterministic menus across contract and skill guidance
